title: orchis incarnata var. sesquipedalis
intro: Orchis incarnata var. sesquipedalis, also known as the wild iris or purple iris, is a perennial plant belonging to the orchid family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Orchis incarnata var. sesquipedalis include its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its use in treating digestive problems and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the roots and rhizomes of the plant are typically dried and used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
